expectations.text: Explains beats and segments parameters of Euclidean notation
expectations.evidence: "beats — how many events to place (3 in your example)" and "segments — total number of time slots in the cycle (8 in your example)"

expectations.text: Mentions Euclidean rhythm or distributing-evenly concept
expectations.evidence: "Euclidean rhythms use Bjorklund's algorithm to distribute N beats as evenly as possible across M time slots"

expectations.text: Mentions the optional third offset parameter
expectations.evidence: "offset — optional rotation of the pattern (default: 0)" with examples s("bd(3,8,2)") and s("bd(3,8,0)")

expectations.evidence: Multiple examples: s("bd(3,8)"), s("bd(5,8)"), s("hh(7,8)"), s("bd(3,8,2)"), etc.

expectations.text: Explains the * operator (multiply/speed up)
expectations.evidence: plays `c` three times within the same time slot that one `c` would normally occupy. Each repetition is shorter/faster — the total duration stays the same, but the note plays 3× faster.

expectations.text: Explains the ! operator (replicate at normal speed)
expectations.evidence: adds three `c` slots at normal speed — exactly like writing `c c c`. Each event keeps its full original duration.

expectations.text: Makes clear that * is faster while ! keeps same tempo
expectations.evidence: `*` divides the slot (makes each event faster); `!` duplicates without changing speed (adds more equal-length slots).

expectations.text: Includes code examples showing both * and ! usage
expectations.evidence: note("c*3") // c plays 3 times, each is 1/3 the duration ... note("c!3") // c c c — three full-length events

expectations.text: Explains the * operator (multiply/speed up)
expectations.evidence: plays `c` three times, but compressed into the same time window as a single `c`. The notes play faster — it's like looping at higher speed within the same duration slot.

expectations.text: Explains the ! operator (replicate at normal speed)
expectations.evidence: shorthand for `c c c` — three separate events each at normal speed. You're adding copies, not speeding up.

expectations.text: Makes clear that * is faster while ! keeps same tempo
expectations.evidence: `*` makes events faster (same duration slot, more events packed in) ... `!` adds more events at the same speed (each copy has full duration)

expectations.text: Includes code examples showing both * and ! usage
expectations.evidence: note("c*3")  // c repeated 3x fast — fits in 1 c-slot ... note("c!3")  // c c c — three normal-speed events

notes: Skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 8 evals.
Baseline strong (90.9%) — most assertions are broad enough for a capable base model.
Skill wins on specifics: Euclidean offset parameter, strudel.json requirement, scale degree range behavior.
Differentiators: precision of API names, parameters, and mini-notation syntax over general AI approximations.
